name: Caffeine
comp: The structure is built on two fused rings sharing an edge: an aromatic six-membered ring and an aromatic five-membered ring (xanthine). The aromatic six-membered ring contains two nitrogen atoms, and four carbon atoms. The aromatic five-membered ring contains two nitrogen atoms, and three carbon atoms. From N1 of the ring, a carbon atom is attached to the ring (bonded to three hydrogens). This forms an N-methyl group (–CH₃). From the ring, a double bond connects to an oxygen atom. This forms a urea linkage (N–C(=O)–N). From N3 of the ring, a carbon atom is attached to the ring (bonded to three hydrogens). This forms an N-methyl group (–CH₃). From the ring, a double bond connects to an oxygen atom. This forms a lactam (cyclic amide, N–C(=O)–C). From N7 of the ring, a carbon atom is attached to the ring (bonded to three hydrogens). This forms an N-methyl group (–CH₃). The remaining ring position, C8, is bonded to an implicit hydrogen atom.
std: A 9-atom fused ring system (xanthine, purine-2,6-dione) with three N-methyl groups. Functional groups: a urea linkage, and a lactam (cyclic amide).
short: A fused ring system (xanthine, purine-2,6-dione) with three N-methyl groups.

From N7 of the ring, a carbon atom is attached to the ring (bonded to three hydrogens). This forms an N-methyl group (–CH₃). From the ring, a double bond connects to an oxygen atom. This forms a lactam (cyclic amide, N–C(=O)–C). From the ring, a double bond connects to an oxygen atom. This forms a urea linkage (N–C(=O)–N). From N3 of the ring, a carbon atom is attached to the ring (bonded to three hydrogens). This forms an N-methyl group (–CH₃). The remaining two ring positions carry implicit hydrogens: N1, and C8.

short: A fused ring system (xanthine, purine-2,6-dione) with two N-methyl groups.

name: Uracil
comp: The structure is centred on a six-membered aromatic ring (pyrimidine), containing two nitrogen atoms, and four carbon atoms. Two branches extend from the ring, at meta positions: Branch 1: From the ring, a double bond connects to an oxygen atom. This forms a lactam (cyclic amide, N–C(=O)–C). comp: The structure is centred on a six-membered aromatic ring (pyrimidine), containing two nitrogen atoms, and four carbon atoms. name: Aspirin
comp: The structure is centred on a six-membered aromatic ring (benzene), with all six ring positions occupied by carbon atoms. Two branches extend from the ring, at adjacent (ortho) positions: Branch 1: From the ring, a single bond connects to an oxygen atom. From this oxygen, a single bond connects to a carbon atom. From this carbon, a single bond connects to a carbon atom (bonded to three hydrogens), and a double bond connects to an oxygen atom. This forms an ester group (–OCOCH₃). Branch 2: A carbon atom is attached to the ring. From this carbon, a double bond connects to an oxygen atom, and a single bond connects to an oxygen atom (bonded to one hydrogen). This forms a carboxylic acid group (–COOH). The remaining four ring positions are each bonded to an implicit hydrogen atom.
std: A 13-atom molecule built on a benzene ring. Two substituents at adjacent (ortho) positions: an ester group (–OCOCH₃), and a carboxylic acid group (–COOH).

name: Naproxen
comp: The structure is built on two fused aromatic six-membered rings sharing an edge (naphthalene). Each ring contains six carbon atoms. From a carbon atom in the ring, a carbon atom is attached to the ring (bonded to one hydrogen). From this carbon, a single bond connects to a carbon atom (bonded to three hydrogens), and a single bond connects to a carbon atom. This forms a carboxylic acid group (–COOH). The remaining six ring positions are each bonded to an implicit hydrogen atom.
